summary: The initiative delivers a browser‑based Sudoku game packaged as a single HTML file that lets users pick a difficulty, fill the grid, track time and mistakes, and see a completion screen. It targets internal demo audiences and must work offline on both desktop and mobile browsers with a responsive UI.

assumptions.text: Puzzles are pre‑loaded as static data within the HTML file (no runtime generation).
The app will run in modern browsers that support ES6 and CSS Grid.
All icons/fonts and assets must be embedded as data URIs to satisfy the single‑file constraint.
The pause/resume UI will be a full‑screen overlay that blacks out the screen.
The duplicate‑highlight toggle does not need to persist after a page reload.

requirementsInterpreted.text: Render a 9x9 Sudoku grid with clear 3x3 sub‑box borders.
Allow the user to start a new puzzle and select difficulty (easy, medium, hard).
Prevent editing of the fixed starting numbers.
Enable cell selection and number entry (1‑9) via keyboard or on‑screen controls.
Highlight the selected row, column, and sub‑box.
Provide a timer that starts on first move, can be paused/resumed via a manual pause control, and stops on puzzle completion.
Show a mistake counter that increments on invalid moves or conflicts.
Display a completion screen with elapsed time, difficulty, and total mistakes.
Include reset and new‑puzzle buttons that preserve the current difficulty.
Package all code, styles, and assets in a single HTML file and operate fully offline.

outOfScope.text: Implementing a dynamic puzzle generator algorithm.
Integration with external analytics or tracking services.
Using external JavaScript libraries or CSS frameworks.

openQuestions.text: How many distinct puzzles per difficulty should be included, and is random selection required?
Must the game state (board, timer, mistakes) survive a page reload or navigation away?
Should the duplicate‑number highlighting toggle persist across sessions?
Is there a required accessibility compliance level (e.g., WCAG AA) beyond the optional ARIA labels?

riskFlags.text: Single‑file constraint may limit future extensibility or addition of assets.
Potential performance degradation on low‑end devices due to all code running in one file.
No network requests means any bug requires a full file rebuild and redeploy.
